title: Same item on multiple boards
excerpt: Need a way for the same item to live on multiple boards, with the changes made in one place (be it notes, status changes, date changes, files uploaded to the info box, etc.) reflecting in the other board. I can’t figure …

posts.author: MikeCarrels (Mike Carrels)
posts.cookedHtml: Need a way for the same item to live on multiple boards, with the changes made in one place (be it notes, status changes, date changes, files uploaded to the info box, etc.) reflecting in the other board. I can’t figure out how to do this.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Mike!

This is possible using our connect boards and mirror column. Had you explored these columns as an option?

posts.author: MikeCarrels (Mike Carrels)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I have, and that’s not what I’m looking for.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Understood! So you’d essentially like to create dynamic items across boards?

posts.author: MikeCarrels (Mike Carrels)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bianca Taylor:

dynamic item

I am not sure what you mean by dynamic items. Let’s say we have one dashboard that is for a project. And another dashboard that is for weekly tasks. So the week I’m working on something for the project, those items would be on the offices’ weekly tasks dashboard.

Right now, you basically have to duplicate the items on both dashboards, typing them all in again, etc., etc. And then designate their status once complete in both places. I’d just like to be able to do it once and it reflect on both dashboards.

posts.author: LouiseS (Louise)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes - agree with Mike - would love to see this feature

posts.author: ClarissaBo (Clarissa)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT I totally agree with @MikeCarrels! I’ve been looking for something similar for so long but the connect boards and mirror columns just aren’t enough.
Think about alias folders in the Finder, that would just be perfect!

posts.author: Ishay_Healthy (Ishay Frenkel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Monday team, don’t over-complicate it.
The item just need to be inter-connected so that if one thing changes on one board, the changes reflect on that same item that appears in a different board.
It should be one item that has 2 or more pointers to it, so it can appear on multiple boards.
Another way to think about it is like an alias of a file.

posts.author: dinhb225 (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: I need this function. I have been searching this function for a long time but have not found the solution

Hopefully the Monday.com Product Team takes this as a priority!

posts.author: ewertepny (Ed Wertepny)
posts.cookedHtml: This doesn’t work for my needs, essentially I need to be able to use a general date column that is a part of task based project board, that can allow for using an “intersection” of data (X&Y coordinate on the board, also the ability to map across templates), to be rolled up to the overview board as a column (various columns that are pertinent to phases in the process and management) and the rows are the projects. The Roll Up App, but I don’t feel that this functionality is something that I should have to pay a third party vendor to use.

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: In that case, I think the VLOOKUP app is a good fit. I don’t think this feature is available with the native monday.com features

posts.cookedHtml: Hi John, the app does exactly that, actually It adds the item you need to another board, and if you change a shared column / post an update, this will be reflected in all other locations of the item. You can test it out yourself, there’s a free plan available!

posts.author: Markochopsticks (Mark Harris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has anyone found a work around here?

I cannot afford to pay the cost of the apps below (although they do exactly what is needed) I just cannot understand how this is not already an existing function in Monday.

I need to have the items in into each project so I can have that clear overview of e everything which needs to happen in order to complete the project - but then I need to be able to manage the working day and week by having those item viewable in a different board, where I can organise tasks from multiple projects into a priority order order and time block. Without this I am finding it impossible to know what to actually work on each day , this seems like a fundamental function of a PM software.

I am also open to suggestions of alternative programs which might offer this as its a non negotiable basic function for me and without it Monday is not viable as a software to manage multiple projects.

Hi Mark,

I completely understand your frustration, and I appreciate you sharing your thoughts. It sounds like our app could be a great fit for your needs, and I’d hate for pricing to be a barrier.

please contact us via chat at sameitem.pioneera.io, and I’ll personally take care of this for you.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

posts.author: adrimaria (Adrineli Canelon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are there any updates on this feature? We need it to be native.
I’ve used the “Same Item Multiple Boards” app and it works well, but the control we have over what to setup is limited and the automations involved in the integration aren’t managed by you really.
Also, it is extra cost for a feature that every decent PM tool has as native, it doesn’t make sense that an organization as big as Monday hasn’t implemented it yet. My clients have Enterprise plan and they still need to pay extra for things other tools do native and better.

posts.author: wednesday.com (David)
posts.cookedHtml: It’'s wild that monday.com has not figured this out yet. honestly holds my company back in so many ways, it’s a massive waste of time having to update the “same” item across multiple boards.

posts.author: cptbrau (Hal)
posts.cookedHtml: We joined Monday.com recently and I’m seriously questioning if we made the right choice when this absolutely core functionality is not included as a native feature. The whole point was to handle all of this in one place, not have to frankenstein external apps that add additional costs and do a bunch of workarounds that don’t do what every other software of this nature does easily.

Snir here from the Same Item Multiple Boards team.

The app has been live for over two years now — and many of our earliest users are still with us today. We’ve continued to refine and expand it to support more complex workflows, without compromising simplicity or reliability.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @MikeCarrels !

We actually built an app specifically for this use case. Mirror Item Multiple Boards lets you:

  • Keep one item synced across unlimited boards
  • Update anywhere, changes reflect everywhere instantly
  • Add subitems, comments, and files in the sync
  • Exclude certain columns
  • See live sync status in using the sync table feature

How it works: You just select an item, choose which boards to mirror it to, and that’s it. Any updates sync automatically in real-time.

You don’t have to manually link anything. Our app automatically checks for common columns and syncs those. A critical feature that we have is to exclude certain common columns from syncing -
- To give you an example, you could have the same item live on multiple boards with different names for each (if you exclude the name column).

Since we just launched, we’re offering 50% off yearly plans for early adopters (around $18/month).

Happy to answer any questions or show a quick demo if helpful! Schedule a call here

121920×960 205 KB

posts.author: AndreaWilhelm (Andrea Wilhelm)
posts.cookedHtml:
Adding in that I would also advocate for including this in the base functionality. While the app developed by garfield-labs technically does this it adds unnecessary overhead and complication to the intuitive way we work across projects.

The work management tool needs to align as closely as possible to the way we execute on our work. Any friction points open up the possibility of users shifting away from using the system.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @AndreaWilhelm ,

You can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

The app works in a simple and easy way -

  • Keep the workflow intuitive and lightweight
  • Reduce overhead instead of adding it
  • Mirror Monday.com’s native behavior as closely as possible, so users don’t feel like they’re working around the system

posts.author: Andoni00 (Andoni)
posts.cookedHtml: Bumping this up as it’s been a couple of years. I understand there are several plug-ins available but it would be great if Monday.com develops this feature natively, similar to Asana and leveraging the idea of one item across multiple boards (as opposed to relying on duplicates, even if there are multiple automations that make this seamless on the front end).
